clos.text: Define an agent as an LLM running in a loop with memory and tools, and identify its four core components (LLM, tools, memory, loop).
Distinguish between a workflow (predetermined control flow via code) and an agent (model-driven control flow) using the test: who decides what happens next?
Analyze the spectrum of agency (single LLM call, tool-augmented LLM, workflow, single agent, multi-agent) and explain why each step increases latency, token cost, and failure modes.
Apply the escalation principle to justify design choices by naming specific failures of simpler architectures rather than invoking edge cases.
Implement an agent loop (think–act–observe–think) that handles constitutional prompts, MCP tool calls, and bounded iteration via max_iterations.
Evaluate the control flow of an agentic system by identifying whether the code or the model steers execution at each decision point.
Use the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to expose a tool catalog to an LLM and interpret structured tool-call requests from the model.
Design a constitutional prompt that shapes agent behavior by specifying role, standards, constraints, and communication tone.
Read an agentic system as a pattern stack by naming Layer 1 patterns (reasoning, tool use, memory, safety), Layer 2 composition, and Layer 3 anti-patterns.

topics.name: Agent anatomy: LLM, tools, memory, loop, and constitutional prompt
Workflow versus agent: control flow and the distinction between predetermined and model-driven execution
Spectrum of agency: single call, tool-augmented, workflow, single agent, multi-agent
Escalation principle: demonstrating failure of simpler designs before adding complexity
Cost of every pattern: latency, tokens, and failure modes
Think–act–observe loop mechanics: LLM decision, MCP tool execution, result feeding
Model Context Protocol (MCP): exposing tool catalogs and handling structured tool requests
Constitutional prompt: role, standards, constraints, and behavioral shaping
Pattern stack model: Layer 1 (agent-level), Layer 2 (system-level), Layer 3 (anti-patterns)
Agent implementation: class structure, async loop, MCP server integration, termination criteria

guiding_principles: Focus on the important, big-picture take-aways from this material.
